content:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.

Statements

Some plates are knives.

Some knives are bottles.

Many bottles are bowls.

Conclusions

I. Some plates are bowls.

II. Not a single plate is bowl.

options.content: If either conclusion I or II follows.

explanation:
According to the question,

Conclusion I and II are complementary pair. Hence, option (d) is correct.

content:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.

Statements

1. Few gentlemen are rich.

2. Few gentlemen are not fools.

3. Everyone is either a fool or rich.

Conclusions

I. No gentleman is rich.

II. Few gentlemen being fools is a possibility.

III. No one is both a fool and rich.
explanation:
According to the statements,

Conclusions I. $$(\times)$$ II. $$(\checkmark)$$ III. $$(\times)$$

So, only Conclusion II follows.

content:
Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.

Statements

1. Some chimpanzees are tigers.

2. All tigers are elephants.

Conclusions

I. Some elephants are tigers.

II. Some elephants are chimpanzees.

options.content: Neither Conclusion I nor II follows
Both Conclusions I and II follow

explanation:
According to the question,

Conclusions I. ($$\checkmark$$) II. $$(\checkmark)$$
